summary: Start M6-POLICY-EVENT-RUNTIME-001: implement deterministic, versioned policy and event runtime substrate through authoritative sim-core command/scheduler paths and controlled content boot boundary.
acceptance_criteria: Policy and event runtime state is explicit, deterministic, versioned, and authoritative only inside sim-core through GameCommand and scheduler paths.

Scenario-defined policy/event definitions compile through content schemas and are consumed through a controlled boot boundary without sim-core importing Node IO, DOM, React, Pixi, Electron, or real time.
Events expose auditable causes, options, consequences, and encyclopedia references without arbitrary-code scripting, hidden callbacks, reflection, or object-graph serialization.
Save/load, replay, rejection, and compatibility coverage prevents policy/event state loss or malformed event injection.
required_tests: Property/invariant tests cover deterministic event ordering, command parity, explicit rejection reasons, malformed definition rejection, and save/replay round-trip.
constraints: Do not edit forbidden paths, especially apps/web, apps/desktop, packages/sim-ai, UI/client packages, content-source, tools, CI, roadmap, gate docs, PROJECT_STATUS, manifests, or validation reports.
Do not introduce arbitrary-code scripting, hidden callbacks, reflection, object-graph serialization, Node IO/DOM/React/Pixi/Electron/real-time imports into sim-core.
Policy/event definitions must be explicit schemas and controlled boot/runtime data, not scriptable callbacks.
Authoritative state changes must occur through GameCommand, rule, scheduler, or system paths; same seed/initial state/commands must produce the same result/hash.
Events must expose auditable causes, options, consequences, reason codes, and encyclopedia references.
Save/load, replay, rejection, malformed definition injection, deterministic ordering, and command parity need focused coverage.
M6 must preserve DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE and must not implement battle UI, full diplomacy, telemetry, server/multiplayer, arbitrary-code mods, or product-direction changes.
requested_action: Implement the task in this branch, run required/focused tests as feasible, write a structured REVIEW handoff to project/messages/outbox, and route back to systems_architect via lead. Do not mark ACCEPTED or CLOSED.
